subject: Fwd: PERMIT TIME TABLE
body:
Regarding the CZM permit for the existing compound, John forwarded it to me  
yesterday and I have given it to Rahnna to fax to you.  I am checking  it 
against the application to make sure that everything requested in the  application 
was granted in the permit. 
 
I note that in the conditions section of the permit, there appears to be a  
fairly onerous reporting and monitoring requirement.  When John sent me the  
permit, he told me that all the conditions were standard.  Because I had  never 
seen that particular onerous condition in any of our other permits, I  asked 
John about it again and he told me that Brathwaite, who was at CZM when  John 
picked up the permit, told John that this condition is what CZM  is doing on 
their permits now.  Obviously, I have no way of knowing whether  John is 
reporting accurately about Brathwaite.
 
Also regarding Brathwaite, John told me that he reminded Brathwaite about  
the outstanding Release from CZM and Brathwaite promised John that Brathwaite  
would get it done as soon as Brathwaite had a chance to do so.
 
Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New  York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: _dkiesq@aol.com_ (mailto:dkiesq@aol.com) 

------------------------------

federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion 
 or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in  this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular  circumstances from an independent tax advisor.  
=================================================================

information from Darren  K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or 
that are otherwise  confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by

who receives  this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, 
distributing, and/or  using it. If you have received this communication in error, 
please immediately  delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at

From: "John Amerling" <john_amerling@earthlink.net>
Subject: PERMIT TIME TABLE
Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2008 15:30:57 GMT
To: DKIESQ@aol.com

Enclosed is the document which you requested. I am faxing it along with this
note. Please note:

 

1.       The durations are fixed on what the regulations require and, in
some cases what the literature concerning filing says will happen without
being bound to do so, such as how long DPNR will take on a Minor decision

2.       There are many places where the dates can slip, i.e., if ACOE
requests more information the times are extended by the amount of time
responding takes

3.       The ACOE can hold a hearing on the application although that is
unusual and, to date, has not in the offing

4.       The federal public notice provisions can be waived, but I think
this very unlikely given the past history and the fact that local Fish &
Wildlife have been so vocal in their criticism of what is going on
on-island. 

5.       This chart is oversimplified: for example a water quality
certification may be required; to date we have been told that the existing
determination on file is sufficient

6.       THE BIG OPPORTUNITY TO HORSE AROUND HERE IT THE PROCESS OF DEEMING
THE APPLICATION COMPLETE; THE OTHER PROBLEM COULD BE THAT DPNR MIGHT SAY
THAT WE SHOULD SUBMIT A MINOR APPLICATION ON A SEPARATE DOCUMENT FROM THE
935; REMEMBER I TOOK UP THESE SUBJECTS WHEN I MET WITH DPNR WITH DAPHNE AND
MONIQUE AND THE ANSWER SEEMED TO BE FILE THE 935 AND THAT WILL DO IT, MAYBE.

 

My billing rate at Sanford & Amerling is currently $ 225 an hour. 

 

Is this e mail and the enclosure sufficient to close out our negotiations?

Lafayette Contractors, LLC

6100 Red Hook Quarters, Suite A2-7

St. Thomas, VI 00802

340 779-8054 (O)

340 779-8055 (FAX)

340 690-7194 (Cell)
body_html:
Regarding the CZM permit for the existing compound, John forwarded it to me yesterday and I have given it to Rahnna to fax to you.  I am checking it against the application to make sure that everything requested in the application was granted in the permit. 
 
I note that in the conditions section of the permit, there appears to be a fairly onerous reporting and monitoring requirement.  When John sent me the permit, he told me that all the conditions were standard.  Because I had never seen that particular onerous condition in any of our other permits, I asked John about it again and he told me that Brathwaite, who was at CZM when John picked up the permit, told John that this condition is what CZM is doing on their permits now.  Obviously, I have no way of knowing whether John is reporting accurately about Brathwaite.
 
Also regarding Brathwaite, John told me that he reminded Brathwaite about the outstanding Release from CZM and Brathwaite promised John that Brathwaite would get it done as soon as Brathwaite had a chance to do so.
 
Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com

1.       The durations are fixed on what the regulations require and, in some cases what the literature concerning filing says will happen without being bound to do so, such as how long DPNR will take on a Minor decision

2.       There are many places where the dates can slip, i.e., if ACOE requests more information the times are extended by the amount of time responding takes
